text.title: django-coding-standards Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for Django repo layout and code conventions.

rule.statement: Repository tree: `apps/<app>/` for domain apps, `core/` for cross-cutting code (BaseModel, mixins, permissions), `config/` for settings (`base.py`, `development.py`, `production.py`, `test.py`). No code at the repo root.
rule.rationale: Predictable layout enables agents and humans to navigate without grep. Repo-root code resists testing and breaks `python -m` invocations.

rule.statement: Cross-app imports MUST be aliased: `from apps.users.models import User as users_models_User`. Direct symbol imports (`from apps.users.models import User`) are forbidden — they clash on common names (User, Status).
rule.rationale: Multiple apps inevitably define `User` or `Status`. Aliasing prevents shadowing and makes the source app explicit at the import site.

rule.statement: Business logic lives in `apps/<app>/services/<topic>.py`. Views call services. Services orchestrate models, utils, and integrations. Tasks are thin wrappers over services. Utils are pure functions.
rule.rationale: Layering keeps each layer testable in isolation. Logic in views / utils / models defeats testability and concentrates churn in the wrong layer.

rule.statement: Service functions declare keyword-only arguments after `*`. `def create_user(*, email: str, name: str, role: Role) -> User:`.
rule.rationale: Positional args produce silent bugs on signature change (swap two args of the same type). Keyword-only forces every callsite to be explicit.

rule.statement: Use `models.TextChoices` (or `IntegerChoices`) for enums. Centralise in `apps/<app>/constants.py`. Never use plain strings for status / kind / role.
rule.rationale: TextChoices give a typed enum, a `.choices` form for Django forms, and a translation-ready label. Plain strings drift and produce typos that bypass validation.

rule.statement: Every `instance.save(update_fields=[...])` lists the changed fields. Bare `instance.save()` is allowed only on creation.
rule.rationale: Bare save() writes all fields; signals fire for unrelated changes; auditing produces noise. `update_fields` makes the write surface explicit and avoids contention.
